perks.desc: No penalty for actions with the off-hand. Two pistols at once. Two blades. Less than 1% from birth.

perks.name: TOUGHNESS

levels.desc: +2/+4/+6 global hits. At TOUGH 50 (10 hits) even +2 is +20% survivability.

perks.vignette:
Colonel Serov took three bullets in the chest, two in the belly and one in the shoulder during a shoot-out in Nizhny Novgorod. By every protocol he should have died in hospital on day three. Serov died on day seventeen — of pneumonia, because the lungs never properly worked. But seventeen days are seventeen days.
When the doctor in intensive care asked him on day five how he was holding on, Serov rasped: «I just don't want to die today. I'll think about tomorrow tomorrow.»
perks.desc: +2/+4/+6 global hits. At TOUGH 50 (10 hits) even +2 is +20% survivability.

perks.name: ACCELERATED REGENERATION

levels.desc: +2/+4/+6 regeneration hits/day. Wounds close two or three times faster.

perks.vignette:
When operative Velikhov came to the doctor for the third time that month with a stitched-up brow, the doctor frowned. «You — back to me three times in two weeks?» — «Everything's fine,» Velikhov shrugged. «I heal like a dog.»
The doctor looked at the stitches. Clean, pink, ready to come out, though he had put them in five days ago.
«A dog doesn't heal like that,» said the doctor. «A dog heals slower.»
Velikhov shrugged again. «So I heal better than a dog. Where's the problem?»
perks.desc: +2/+4/+6 regeneration hits/day. Wounds close two or three times faster.

perks.name: ALACRITY

levels.desc: +2 AP per round. One extra action. *(Cost 12, not 3 — in a synchronous system every AP is critical.)*

perks.vignette:
When Malakhov entered a room, in one round he managed: to shoot the nearest, step over the body, reload, fire at the second and kick an overturned stool so that it covered the doorway. None of the rest of the group managed more than two shots in the same round.
Malakhov was not faster. He simply did one action more — every time, the whole operation. By the end of combat he had used five extra rounds-worth of action that the others did not. Five rounds in a synchronous fight is an eternity.
perks.desc: +2 AP per round. One extra action. *(Cost 12, not 3 — in a synchronous system every AP is critical.)*

perks.name: FINE BLADE

levels.desc: Extended critical zone: 1–15 and 85–100 (instead of 1–5 and 95–100). Doubled successes and doubled failures. You are a person of extremes. When it works, it works brilliantly. When it doesn't, catastrophe. Life on the edge — in both directions.

perks.vignette:
Player Renzo at the Grandmaster's table threw three times in a row below 15. Three critical successes. A dagger entering exactly through the armour gap. Parrying a bastard's blow with a one-handed blade. A throwing knife severing a crossbow's bowstring.
The fourth throw — 97. Critical failure. Renzo tripped on his own cloak and fell face-first onto his own dagger.
Fine blade. Cuts both ways.
perks.desc: Extended critical zone: 1–15 and 85–100 (instead of 1–5 and 95–100). Doubled successes and doubled failures. You are a person of extremes. When it works, it works brilliantly. When it doesn't, catastrophe. Life on the edge — in both directions.

perks.name: DANGER SENSE

levels.desc: A warning of danger an instant before it lands. d10: succeeds on 1–2 / 1–4 / 1–6. Hairs on the nape. A cold finger down the spine. «Don't go in there.» It doesn't explain what or why — it just is. Trust it.

perks.vignette:
Grigoryev stopped on the threshold of the flat. Ordinary flat. Nine-storey building. Third floor. Case of poltergeist — furniture flying, neighbours complaining, the woman of the house in hysterics. Routine.
Grigoryev had no paranormal abilities. None. He was an ordinary detective with an ordinary brain and thirty years' experience. But on the threshold of this ordinary flat — the hairs on his nape stood up.
He stepped back. Said to the group: «Wait outside.» Called the OPI scanner. The scanner read the flat — and the meter went off the chart. The «poltergeist» was a higher-class entity, materialising from a thin world. Three operatives in the flat would have died in the first thirty seconds.
When Grigoryev was asked later how he knew, he shrugged. «The skin felt it. I didn't.»
perks.desc: A warning of danger an instant before it lands. d10: succeeds on 1–2 / 1–4 / 1–6. Hairs on the nape. A cold finger down the spine. «Don't go in there.» It doesn't explain what or why — it just is. Trust it.

perks.name: LUCKY

levels.desc: Re-roll one failed roll per session. By the master's call — can be used at a critical moment. Doesn't guarantee success: just a second chance. Sometimes a second chance is all you need.

perks.vignette:
Serov was aiming at a cultist. Hood, black robe, ritual knife in hand — classic possessed, a stranger from a thin world in a human body. Behind the cultist — Katya Sheremetyeva, tied to the ritual pillar. The sight rested on the cultist's head. Finger — on the trigger.
And then Serov SAW it.
Not with his eyes. With something else — what OPI couldn't measure but called «the sense of the moment». In the second the trigger gave, the cultist would step back. The bullet would go past him — into Katya.
Serov took his finger off the trigger. Lowered the GSh-18 by two centimetres. Fired into the cultist's knee.
The cultist fell. The trick worked — though Serov could not say how he knew, in advance, that the cultist would step back. Katya was untied that same evening. Serov, signing the report, wrote: «Took the shot on the knee on intuition.»
The operations head circled the word «intuition» in red ink and added in the margin: «You don't shoot on intuition, Serov. You shoot on luck. And luck likes you.»
perks.desc: Re-roll one failed roll per session. By the master's call — can be used at a critical moment. Doesn't guarantee success: just a second chance. Sometimes a second chance is all you need.

perks.name: DESTINY'S CHILD

levels.desc: +20 to a critically important skill at a critical moment. Once per session. When all is lost, when failure means death — fate winks. Not magic. Not divine intervention. Just... lucky. Or: someone watches over you. Up to the master.

perks.vignette:
A warehouse. Darkness. A rusalka — not the fairy-tale kind, with tails and shells. The real kind: grey skin, black eyes without pupils, a mouth that opens to a hundred and eighty degrees and is full of teeth, each like a fishhook. She was singing. Infrasound, at the edge of hearing — a vibration in the ribs, nausea, clouding of consciousness.
Volkov on the floor, on all fours, vomiting onto the concrete. Sheremetyeva pressed against the wall, the silver dagger in her hand jerking erratically. Two more operatives — out.
Serov was last on his feet. He couldn't think. He couldn't aim. He fired by feel — without sights, without bracing, just turned the gun toward the singing and pulled the trigger.
The round went into the rusalka's left eye. Through the optic nerve. Into the centre of the skull.
A dying rusalka stops singing.
OPI later wrote: «Probability of such a hit blindly, in a state of incipient unconsciousness, on a moving target — under 0.01%.»
Serov, when he read the report, shrugged. «I just had to. So it happened.»
perks.desc: +20 to a critically important skill at a critical moment. Once per session. When all is lost, when failure means death — fate winks. Not magic. Not divine intervention. Just... lucky. Or: someone watches over you. Up to the master.

perks.name: STEEL CORE

levels.desc: **6:** In an ethical wavering — the «right» decision comes by itself. Not morals — a core. You don't agonise over the choice: your internal compass works. Others doubt. You don't. **12:** People feel it. The voice carries weight. Subordinates accept your decisions without question. **24:** A force-of-nature presence. Where you go, even higher powers stop and listen.

perks.vignette:
Group «Epsilon» entered the school building on the twelfth minute of the operation. A Satanist cult — real, not dressed-up, not nuts with chalk pentagrams — had holed up in the assembly hall with hostages. Forty-three children. Seven teachers.
When «Epsilon» kicked the doors — there was no hall. Instead — a well. The walls fanned downward like a funnel, and at the bottom of that funnel sat a Thing with no name in any human language. The cult had opened a Gate.
Lieutenant Yermakov, who had taken command after the wounding of the captain, looked into the well. The Thing looked back.
A voice without a mouth said: «What do you want?»
Yermakov did not look away. Did not waver. Did not seek words. From inside, from where the steel core was, came: «The children.»
The Thing was silent. Then said: «Pay.»
«No,» said Yermakov.
«Pay or all die.»
«No.»
The Thing closed. The Gate folded. The hall returned. The children came out alive.
What exactly had Yermakov done — OPI could not establish.
perks.desc: **6:** In an ethical wavering — the «right» decision comes by itself. Not morals — a core. You don't agonise over the choice: your internal compass works. Others doubt. You don't. **12:** People feel it. The voice carries weight. Subordinates accept your decisions without question. **24:** A force-of-nature presence. Where you go, even higher powers stop and listen.

perks.name: SEX APPEAL

levels.desc: +3/+6/+9 to seduction and social skills. Not beauty — magnetism.

perks.vignette:
Interrogator Roshchina was no beauty — the nose too long, the chin too heavy. But when she entered a room, men forgot what they had been saying. Something in the movements, in the voice, in the way she tilted her head while listening — as if every word of yours was the most important she'd heard that week.
Where her male colleagues sent in an arrest team, dragging the suspect in by the collar, Roshchina went in herself. With coffee. Two cups. Sat across from the suspect, listened. Half an hour. An hour. Two hours.
By the third hour the suspect was confessing. Not because she had threatened. Because in front of her one couldn't lie. One didn't want to.
perks.desc: +3/+6/+9 to seduction and social skills. Not beauty — magnetism.

perks.name: INFLUENTIAL FRIENDS

levels.desc: An ally to whom one can turn for a favour. Not free help — a return favour follows. **6:** local authority, captain of the guard. **12:** patrician, guild head. **24:** one of the High.

perks.vignette:
Blacksmith Taddeo had forged swords in the Lower City for thirty years. Not a great master — an honest craftsman. Swords sturdy, without flourishes, affordable to legionnaire and mercenary.
One night a wounded man stumbled into the workshop. Young, in an expensive but torn cloak. Behind him — three with blades. Taddeo asked no questions. Grabbed a hammer — not a war hammer, a smith's, two poods of weight — and went to the door.
The three saw the smith with the hammer. The smith was big. The hammer was bigger. The three left.
The young man was Lorenzo dei Conti, second son of the Patrician House Conti. He gave Taddeo his ring. «If you ever need the House Conti,» he said, «show this.»
Taddeo carried the ring fifteen years. Never used it. When his daughter Caterina was abducted by slavers from the Archipelago — Taddeo took the ring to the Conti palazzo.
Three weeks later Caterina came home. Alive. The slavers — gone. Taddeo asked the smith next door whether he heard what had happened on the Archipelago. The smith next door said the half of the Archipelago had been visited by something black, big and unforgiving. They didn't go into details.
perks.desc: An ally to whom one can turn for a favour. Not free help — a return favour follows. **6:** local authority, captain of the guard. **12:** patrician, guild head. **24:** one of the High.

perks.name: WEALTH

levels.desc: Starting resources and lifestyle. **6:** comfortable. **12:** rich. **24:** one of the richest.

perks.vignette:
Amir al-Budati al-Sindbad — not his real name, but the only one under which he was known from the Archipelago to the Ice Lands — owned three ships, two caravanserais, a spice warehouse on the Royal Pier and, by rumour, a controlling interest in half the debts of the Merchant Quarter.
When a mercenary group needed equipment for an expedition to the Archipelago — to hunt a sea-serpent for the Patrician House Veneto — Amir was the only address. The group came in their best clothes. Amir sat on cushions and drank a black-red tea no one else in the city knew.
«Equipment for an expedition?» he repeated. «Of course. A list, please.» He read the list. Nodded once.
«All. By tomorrow evening at the Royal Pier. The price — twelve thousand gold ducats.»
The group commander coughed. «We don't have twelve thousand.»
Amir smiled. «I know. You will pay half from the serpent's head. The other half — at thirty per cent annual. With a guarantee of the House Veneto.»
The commander did not ask how Amir knew about the contract with the House Veneto. With Amir one didn't ask such things.
perks.desc: Starting resources and lifestyle. **6:** comfortable. **12:** rich. **24:** one of the richest.

perks.name: MAN OF THE WORLD

levels.desc: An intuitive feeling for the etiquette and customs of any culture. You didn't study them — you feel them. Walk into another's temple — you know what is not allowed. Sit at a table with strangers — you give no offence. Meet a foreign people — you know how to greet them.

perks.vignette:
Lorenzo's schooner broke up on the rocks three leagues off the coast of the Ice Lands. Two days on the wreckage. When he was thrown ashore — half-dead, salty, blue.
The Children of Ice found him at dawn. Three figures — each a head taller than a Eternal-City man, in furs and bone, faces painted with blue clay. They did not kill strangers — that was a southerner's tale they themselves found amusing — but they did not welcome them either.
Lorenzo, half-conscious, did the right thing. Did not stand up. Did not raise his hands. Did not speak. Sat in the snow, his eyes lowered, and waited.
The eldest of the three crouched beside him. Took a fistful of snow, pressed it to his lips. Lorenzo took the snow. Did not eat. Held in his palm until it melted.
The eldest nodded. «Stranger from the south,» he said in broken Ancient. «You know our custom. Live.»
Lorenzo stayed with the Children of Ice three months. Came home with stories that paid for the loss of the schooner three times over. He never could say how he had known about the snow. «Felt it,» he answered. «There was no other way.»
perks.desc: An intuitive feeling for the etiquette and customs of any culture. You didn't study them — you feel them. Walk into another's temple — you know what is not allowed. Sit at a table with strangers — you give no offence. Meet a foreign people — you know how to greet them.

perks.name: TOXIC IMMUNITY

levels.desc: +25% / +50% / full immunity to poisons.

perks.vignette:
Ghulbsh of the Second Circle did not understand the problem. The little people stood beyond the door in their ridiculous costumes with proboscises — as if a thin layer of fabric could save you from what he breathed. Ghulbsh was small of stature, stooped, lean. No claws, no fangs, no muscles — why bother, if one exhale kills fifty warm-blooded? He licked his dry lips and waited. Let the buncha come in. Let them be heroes. Heroes die quickly. The cheaper the heroism — the quicker.
One of those in the proboscises came in. Without a mask. Tall, thin, in an ORPP combat suit but without the gas mask. He smelled the air. Looked at Ghulbsh.
Ghulbsh exhaled. A wave of foul air rolled through the corridor.
The little one without a mask said: «Smells like a sewer with a corpse.» Casually. As though commenting on the weather.
Then he shot Ghulbsh in the head.
OPI later asked operative Boldyrev how he was alive. Boldyrev shrugged. «I'm immune. Mum drank from puddles in the Kemerovo region. Said I'd grow up — there'd be no problem with poisons.» Boldyrev's mother had been right.
perks.desc: +25% / +50% / full immunity to poisons.

perks.name: BIOLOGICAL IMMUNITY

levels.desc: Immune to disease: ordinary / serious / all including paranormal.

perks.vignette:
Plague came to the Lower City on a trading ship from the Budati Caliphate. By the third day the streets of the Fish Quarter were piled with bodies — alive and dead, because the difference was no longer made. Healers in masks with long beaks stuffed with herbs did not go south of the Third Wall. The watch — also. The gates of the Lower were closed and nailed. Who was inside — was inside.
Doctor Beretta walked the streets of the Lower without a mask. He didn't have one. He had a leather bag with what tools he had left, a flask of plum brandy that wasn't doing the patients any good but kept Beretta on his feet, and a calm that, in his line of work, was a separate diagnosis.
Beretta saw the plague three times in his life. Was infected twice. Once recovered. The second time — survived without recovering: never developed symptoms. His mother had been from the Marshes, where in childhood she had eaten anything that didn't run away. Beretta had inherited her stomach and, with it, a stomach for diseases.
In the Lower City Beretta did not save the city — no doctor could have. He saved seventeen specific people. Without a mask. While the watch stood on the wall and crossed itself.
perks.desc: Immune to disease: ordinary / serious / all including paranormal.

perks.name: EXTERNAL VISION

levels.desc: Spontaneous, uncontrollable vision of the hidden. A gift or a curse.
levels.mechanics: d10 in key scenes (secretly, by the master). 1–2 — a visual hint.

perks.vignette:
He had long forgotten his name. Long — how long? In his world there was no time. There were walls he remembered. There were rooms that had once belonged to him. There were windows beyond which the light changed — that, it seemed, was called «days», but he wasn't sure.
He guessed he was dead. He vaguely remembered the words «paradise» and «hell» — the first good, the second bad. Neither did he see. He saw a corridor. The corridor turned. Behind the turn — a door. Behind the door — another corridor.
Sometimes the corridors lengthened. Sometimes they shortened. He walked them, because what else does one do.
Once — once for the first time in time-that-was-not-time — he saw a person at the end of a corridor. A living one. He knew it was alive because around it was warmth, and warmth he had not seen in this world for a long time. The person stood, looked at a wall as if it could see through.
He came up. The person — a woman, dark-haired, with grey eyes — turned. Looked at him. He thought she would not see. But she did.
«Hello,» she said. «I'm Olga.»
He couldn't remember his name. Said: «I think I'm dead.»
Olga nodded. «Yes. But you can help me. Tell me what you see.»
He said. About corridors. About walls. About a door that he could not pass through but behind which there was a light. About a child — strange, since he had not seen a child for a long time — somewhere far in a corridor he did not enter.
Olga listened. Wrote. Thanked. Said: «You helped. The child is alive.»
Then she went away. He continued to walk. But now in the corridors he sometimes saw light at the ends. Not always. But more often.
perks.desc: Spontaneous, uncontrollable vision of the hidden. A gift or a curse.

perks.name: SAFE

levels.desc: Innate immunity to direct paranormal influence. A telepath will not read your thoughts. A pyrokinetic will not set you on fire. A demon will not possess you. A mental attack breaks on you like a wave on a rock. You give off no aura, do not show on scanners, do not bleed psi.
levels.mechanics: Full immunity to: telepathy, pyrokinesis, biokinetics, possession, mental attacks, mind control, psi-scanning. Vulnerable to: telekinesis on inanimate objects (a thrown stone hurts), physical paranormal effects (a vampire still bites, a werewolf still claws).

perks.vignette:
The mansion on Kamennoostrovsky Avenue showed itself yesterday. Thirty years — an ordinary house, a family, renovations, children. And then — in a single night — a haunting killed the father and the elder daughter. The wife and the two younger ones tore out through a window and called SiB.
Group «Gamma» went in by four. Twenty minutes in, three were down. Grigoryev — mental crush, glassy eyes. Serov — mental crush, looped, walking in circles muttering the same sentence. Volkov — paralysis, ankles locked, lay in the corridor on his back, eyes alive.
One was left. Captain Demidov. Demidov took two steps into the parlour. The mansion swept its presence over him.
Nothing.
Demidov was a Safe. The mansion didn't feel him. The mansion's pressure didn't pass through him.
Demidov walked through three floors, found the icon-shaped artefact that anchored the haunting in the wall behind the chimney, ripped it out and broke it in the kitchen sink under cold water. The mansion died.
The operatives came round one by one. Sheremetyeva fixed Volkov's ankles. The mansion was sealed and turned over to OPI for vivisection.
Demidov never knew what the others had felt. They had tried to describe it. He had listened. Nodded. Could not imagine.
perks.desc: Innate immunity to direct paranormal influence. A telepath will not read your thoughts. A pyrokinetic will not set you on fire. A demon will not possess you. A mental attack breaks on you like a wave on a rock. You give off no aura, do not show on scanners, do not bleed psi.
